degraded.description: If true, try to continue even if some stats are unavailable. In this case, errors will be present in the per-key returned data.

desc_info.description: Shows the description for the specified key(s).

devid.description: Node devid to query. Either an <integer> or "all". Can be used more than one time to query multiple nodes. "all" queries all up nodes. 0 means query the local node. For "cluster" scoped keys, in any devid including 0 can be used.

key.description: One key name. Can be used more than one time to query multiple keys. Also works with 'keys' arguments.

keys.description: Multiple key names. Can be a comma separated list, or can be used more than one time to make queries for multiple keys. May be used in conjunction with 'substr'. Also works with 'key' arguments.

nodes.description: Specify node(s) for which statistics should be reported. One or more comma separated <integer(s)> specifying which node(s) to query, or "all". Specifying more than one node may have unspecified results for keys that begin with "cluster".

raw.description: Causes the output to be in hex format. For internal use only, please do not use this.

show_nodes.description: Shows the logical node number or LNN in addition to the devid.

stale.description: For internal use only, please do not use this.

substr.description: Used in conjunction with the 'keys' argument, alters the behavior of keys. Makes the 'keys' arg perform a partial match. Defaults to false.

timeout.description: Time in seconds to wait for results from remote nodes.

type_info.description: The type ID is used by internal services. For internal use only, please do not use this.

resource_description: This resource allows querying of OneFS current statistics. Current statistics are the most currently available metrics to the time of the request. For a list of available statistics keys with descriptions, see the /1/statistics/keys or /1/statistics/keys/<KEY> resources. Each statistic may be cached for some number of seconds; requerying within this cache period will return the original (cached) value for the statistic.
